title: triticum aestivum subsp. compactum
intro: Triticum aestivum subsp. compactum, also known as emmer wheat, is a subspecies of the domesticated wheat plant, characterized by its compact spikelets and robust growth habit. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, to treat various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. Emmer wheat has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, improving blood sugar control, and promoting digestive health. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and flour, which can be used to make bread, pasta, and other baked goods.
